message:
fix(db/reconstruct): keep recipients_json visible when agent row is missing

Follow-on to b9d5fd4c and 0f21ed96 (tolerate orphaned rows in sync
and mail_explorer).  The last place that still silently dropped
message_recipients rows when the target agent row was gone was
sync_reconstructed_message_recipients_json() in the reconstruct path.

Before: an INNER JOIN against agents meant any recipient whose
agent row had been deleted (by a crash, an incomplete migration, or
an earlier over-eager "clean orphaned rows" doctor pass) vanished
from the reconstructed recipients_json payload on the messages row.
Callers of rebuild/reconstruct would then see "Bob sent a message
to nobody" instead of a correct record of what actually happened.

After:
- LEFT JOIN agents so the recipient row is always emitted.
- Synthesize a placeholder display name via
  `COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.name), ''), '[unknown-agent-' || mr.agent_id || ']')`
  so the JSON still carries a human-readable token that encodes
  which agent_id the row pointed at.
- Simplify the ORDER BY to use the same COALESCE expression as the
  SELECT list, so ordering is well-defined even when a.name is NULL.

Tests:
- New sync_reconstructed_message_recipients_json_keeps_orphaned_recipient_rows_visible
  exercises the fallback path: create an agent, attach it as a
  recipient, delete the agent row, run the sync function, and assert
  that the rebuilt recipients_json contains `["[unknown-agent-7]"]`
  in the `to` list with `cc`/`bcc` empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
